hero.question: Will the US confirm that aliens exist before 2027?
hero.prob_take: At 18.5%, the crowd is pricing alien confirmation as roughly a 1-in-5 shot by 2027, which means they're betting against any major disclosure despite 3+ years of congressional pressure and UAP reporting requirements already in the pipeline. The real tell is volume at $17.8M, suggesting smart money sees this as either artificially cheap (governments crack under transparency demand) or artificially expensive (official confirmation remains politically radioactive forever).

daily_take.headline: Alien Confirmation Odds Jump to 18.5%. Smart Money Spotting Signal or Noise?
daily_take.deck: The UFO disclosure market ticked up 1 point overnight on $17.8M total volume, a modest move that signals real conviction from someone. The 18.5% price still looks cheap if you believe recent Pentagon leaks and congressional pressure actually force a confirmation before 2027, but the timing risk is brutal. Contrarians should ask why this moved now and whether volume is genuine or just tourists piling in on the meme.
